LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MIKE ABELL'S DEMURRER TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CHARLIE YU-HWA PA'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (ENTITLED "AMEND TO COMPLAINT") DOES NOT ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A FRAUD CLAIM. MR. PA IS GIVEN ONE MORE OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE A VIABLE FRAUD CLAIM AGAINST MR. ABELL, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. THE COURT WILL PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY ANN DEYOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK).

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MIKE P. ABELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS). THE COURT WILL PREPARE A WRITTEN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR #59761, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charlie Yu-Hwa Pa (415.452.6315) appears in Propria Persona as self-represented plaintiff/non-opposing party

Mike P. Abell, Principal Engineer of City Structural, Inc (415.696.6323) appears In Propria Persona as self-represented defendant/demurring party

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT, MIKE ABELL'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT of CHARLIE YU-HWA PA is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Second Amended Complaint fails to allege a viable fraud cause of action against Mr. Abell. (CCP 430.10(e).) If defendant Abell seeks to recover his costs, then he is required to comply with the costs memorandum procedure of California Rule of Court 3.1700. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Charlie Yu-Hwa Pa telephone number 415-452-6315 in Propria Persona.
Defendant Mike Abell telephone number 415-696-6323 in Propria Persona.

The matter was discussed. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Mike Abell's demurrer to the sole cause of action for fraud in the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff Charlie Yu-Hwa Pa's first amended complaint (entitled "Amend to Complaint") does not allege sufficient facts to support a fraud claim. Mr. Pa is given one more opportunity to allege a viable fraud claim against Mr. Abell, if he can do so in good faith. The Court will prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charlie Yu-Hwa Pa (415-452-6315) appears as plaintiff/opposing party, In Propria Persona

Mike P. Abell of City Structural, Inc. (415-696-6323) appears as defendant/demurring party, In Propria Personal

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court reads and considers an OBJECTION AND STRUCK TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER that bears the file endorsement of August 29th and treats is as having been properly filed, even though it is not reflected in the Register of Actions. The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MIKE P. ABELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend. Court finds that Mr. Abell is right, that there has not been a proper allegation of fraud. However, Mr. Yu-Hwa Pa, is given an opportunity to correct this deficiency, if he can do so in good faith. Plaintiff must state, if he can, some kind of fraud other than negligent failure to state incompetence or failure to disclose incompetence, he has to say explicit misrepresentation, some statement of fact that Mr. Abell actually made or something that he promised and that he didn't deliver. Court instructs plaintiff regarding leave to amend as reflected in the transcript of the court reporter and as follows: Plaintiff must follow the rules of pleading with regard to fraud. Plaintiff has until September 27, 2016 to file first amended complaint to state an accurate and legally sufficient fraud claim, if he can do so. Court suggests to plaintiff In Pro Per, to make things easier, just bring a breach of contract claim, and deal with it in small claims court, but is not required do that. Otherwise, plaintiff will to have to explain damages in excess of $7500, the small claims limit? Plaintiff will need to do two things (1) Allege a legally sufficient fraud claim and (2) allege why he has been damaged in an amount greater than $7500. The Court will prepare a written order.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
